claim_under_attack: The 18-month retention requirement creates a hard conflict with a publicly committed trust-differentiator privacy policy that cannot be resolved within product scope — executive and legal escalation is required before any implementation work begins.
evidence_or_reason: The visible record supports a policy conflict, but not the stronger claim that it is a 'hard' conflict that bars any implementation work. No evidence is provided that the 18-month requirement is irreducible, that privacy-preserving alternatives were ruled out, or that product/engineering cannot do bounded exploratory work inside the existing 90-day limit while escalation proceeds. The artifact moves from acknowledged uncertainty to a categorical process conclusion without showing why narrower prototyping or alternative designs are non-viable.
